Complete Opposition Research Dossier

KNOW THE APC
Every Aspirant. Every Vulnerability.

A complete map of the All People's Congress flagbearer field, factional structure, financial liabilities, court records, and recommended attack lines — with full source bibliography.

April 2026 15+ Candidate Profiles 9 Parts + Sources SLPP Internal Use All Open-Source
Executive Summary Part I · Field Part II · Koroma Part III · Liabilities Tier 1 Tier 2 Tier 3 Tier 4 Strategy Attack Matrix Sources
EXECExecutive Summary

The APC is unready for national leadership because of its poor and low standards of internal management and administration which are both unimpressive for and about leading any country.

— Seton During, Sierra Leone Telegraph, July 2025

The All People's Congress enters the 2026 National Delegates Conference politically wounded, financially strained, factionally fractured, and constitutionally contested. Two consecutive election losses, an unresolved Section 47(a)(iv) leadership vacancy, an undelivered Chancery judgement against the most recent flagbearer, a former president under treason indictment in exile in Nigeria, and a Commission of Inquiry process that named senior figures for unexplained wealth have produced a field of more than fifteen flagbearer aspirants — none of whom carry both clean institutional credentials and a clean delegate base.

This dossier maps the field in full. For each aspirant the document records origin and bio, full career, declared platform, faction alignment, demonstrated strengths, documented vulnerabilities, and recommended attack lines for SLPP deployment. A dedicated Part on Ernest Bai Koroma anchors the historical context. Three deep reports on the Petroleum Directorate (COI Chapter 9), the New York Chancery procurement case, and Mayor Aki-Sawyerr's donor and partnership map provide the substantive financial spine.

The single most consequential data point

In December 2025 the Institute for Governance Reform published its Sierra Leone's Political Marathon: Internal Democracy of Political Parties — Volume One. The nationwide survey conducted from 9 to 14 December found that only one potential 2028 flagbearer across either party crossed the 50% endorsement threshold: Sheikh Alhaji Mohamed Kamara, popularly known as Jagaban.

Figure · Jagaban's 50%+ result — the only candidate across both parties above the majority line.

Money cannot buy legacy or wisdom — and a candidate who has held no elected or appointed public office cannot run a country.

Six conclusions

  • No APC candidate is invulnerable. Each of the front-rank aspirants — Jagaban, Aki-Sawyerr, Mansaray, Chericoco, Macauley, Konteh, Sam Sumana — carries at least one severe and at least three moderate vulnerabilities documented in open sources.
  • The Northern hegemony coalition is fragmenting. Aspirants from the Eastern Province (Jagaban, Sam Sumana, Konomanyi, Bangura) and from the Western Area (Aki-Sawyerr, Macauley, Blyden, Timbo) now contest space the APC has historically reserved for Temne and Limba Northerners.
  • Section 47(a)(iv) is unresolved. Samura Kamara was removed as National Leader in October–November 2024 under the six-months-after-loss provision. Whoever wins the 2026 convention captures the Leader role — but a 2032 loss bars them in the same way.
  • The Dumbuya detention is the active rule-of-law fault line. Sec-Gen Lansana Dumbuya detained 12 February 2026 and remanded to Pademba Road; proceedings unresolved as of April 2026.
  • The Chancery judgement is the sleeper risk. Closing arguments concluded 12 July 2024; judgement reserved; if delivered before the convention the result reshapes the field overnight.
  • Koroma is the gravity well. Even from Abuja, the former president shapes the field. APC candidates are calibrating their proximity to him in real time.
Discipline noteEvery claim in this dossier is sourced. Allegations against named individuals must not be deployed publicly without primary documentary corroboration. Several lines are flagged with explicit caveats — those caveats must travel with the line.
↑ Top
PART I
The Playing Field

How the APC fractured between 2018 and 2026, and where the lines run today

The APC stands at a dangerous crossroads. Ignoring its loyal members while rewarding opportunists will only lead to political irrelevance.

— Sierra Leone Telegraph, 'The APC Trap,' July 2025

The APC was founded in 1960 by Siaka Stevens — nine years after the SLPP (founded 1951 by Milton Margai). The SLPP was the party of independence; the APC came to power in 1967–68. Between 2007 and 2018 the APC formed the government under President Ernest Bai Koroma. In April 2018 the party lost the presidency to the SLPP's Julius Maada Bio in a runoff. In June 2023 the SLPP won re-election in a contested first-round result. Two consecutive losses, the Tripartite Reform process, and a wave of post-2023 prosecutions of senior APC figures have produced a party in structural disarray.

The factional map

Figure · APC factional alignments — bubble size approximates delegate reach.

  • NAC establishment — centred on National Chairman Mansaray. Includes Joseph Fitzgerald Kamara, Osman Yansaneh. Reportedly orchestrating Mansaray's flagbearer path.
  • Kamara loyalists — centred on Dr. Samura Kamara. Includes Dr. Kaifala Marah, Kemoh Sesay. The Organiser is their media vehicle.
  • Unity dialogue / insurgent technocrats — Jagaban, Macauley, Konteh, Aki-Sawyerr, Sam Sumana, Chericoco. Met in early-January 2026 leadership dialogue.
  • Independent / wildcard — Bangura (DIB Movement), Blyden, Konomanyi, Palo Conteh, Timbo. Konomanyi's March 2026 endorsement of Bangura is the most consequential factional move of the cycle.

The political timeline

Figure · APC political timeline 2013–2026. Red = adverse; Green = favourable/vindication; Navy = procedural/electoral.

Read this chart as showing directionThe APC has a real vindication narrative — multiple COI findings overturned, multiple acquittals — that SLPP attacks must engage rather than ignore. The strongest SLPP ground is the asset-acquisition findings and the Chancery indictment, not the process.

The APC's single strongest argument is the 2023 election credibility deficit. The Carter Center concluded it was not confident the results accurately reflected the will of the people. The EU EOM flagged statistical inconsistencies. NEW's PRVT found Bio did not clear the 55% threshold. Any SLPP attack strategy must acknowledge these findings rather than ignore them — or risk losing credibility with international and educated domestic audiences.

↑ Top
PART II
Ernest Bai Koroma

The unwritten dossier — assets, COI findings, the November 2023 attempted coup, and the Abuja exile

Although officially retired from politics, he remains an influential figure within his political party.

— Al Jazeera, January 2024

Origin and bio

  • Born 2 October 1953 in Makeni, Bombali District, Northern Province.
  • Family. Father Sylvanus Koroma was Temne and Loko, a Wesleyan Bible school teacher. Mother Alice Koroma was Biriwa Limba from Kamabai — women's APC leader in Bombali District, elected to Makeni city council under Siaka Stevens.
  • Ethnicity. Temne with Limba and Loko heritage. Fluent in Temne and Limba.
  • Education. Magburaka Government Secondary School for Boys; Fourah Bay College, Diploma in Business Management, 1976.
  • Wife. Sia Nyama Koroma, ethnically Kono. Biochemist and psychiatric nurse. Daughter of attorney and former Attorney-General Abu Aiah Koroma. Married 18 October 1986.
  • Pre-political career. Teacher at St. Francis Secondary School, Makeni (1976–78). Sierra Leone National Insurance Company from 1978 — overlapping with current National Chairman Mansaray. Joined Reliance Insurance Trust Corporation (Ritcorp) in 1985; Managing Director 1988–2002.

Political ascent

  • 24 March 2002: elected APC leader, defeating Edward Turay at a contested convention.
  • 2002 presidential election: lost decisively to Kabbah (SLPP), 22.35% to 70.3%.
  • 2007 presidential election: won the runoff against SLPP's Solomon Berewa. Sworn in 17 September 2007.
  • 2012 re-election with 58%+ — landslide.
  • 13 April 2022: retired from APC chairmanship and active politics. Office of the Former President released a formal statement he 'has no interest whatsoever in party or national office.'

Commission of Inquiry findings

  • Femi Turner Drive property. Commission valued the asset at approximately USD 5,069,000 (Le 40.5 billion at the time). Concluded the property could not have been acquired from declared presidential income.
  • Mines Chapter 8. Le 63 billion in misappropriated mining revenues cited. Recommended five-year bar from public office for Mansaray, Abdul Ignosi Koroma, Samura Kamara, Fatmata Mustapha, Sahr Wonday, John Bonoh Sisay.
  • Petroleum Chapter 9. Koroma named alongside Raymond Kargbo, Osho Coker, Karefa Kargbo, Momodu L. Kargbo, Michael S. Kargbo, Kaifala Marah. Court of Appeal upheld findings against Koroma and Raymond Kargbo; overturned against Marah and Momodu Kargbo.
  • GTT fiscal accounting. Country effectively bankrupt at handover in April 2018 despite USD 1.3 billion in external assistance received during the decade and a USD 1.6 billion iron ore export boom in 2014.
Justice Bankole Thompson dissentJustice John Bankole Thompson, who chaired Commission Number 2, publicly stated the COI was being misused — findings tantamount to convictions without criminal-trial procedural protections. His dissent is genuine, sourced, and Sierra Leonean. Any SLPP deployment of COI findings must anticipate this rebuttal.

The November 2023 attempted coup and treason case

  • 26 November 2023: gunmen attacked Wilberforce Barracks, Pademba Road Prison, and other locations in Freetown. More than 20 people killed. Approximately 2,200 inmates freed from prison.
  • December 2023: Koroma summoned for questioning. Approximately 40 hours interrogated by CID and ACC.
  • 3 January 2024: charged with four offences — treason, misprision of treason, and two counts of harbouring. Life imprisonment maximum. Bail granted.
  • Late January 2024: High Court granted permission to travel to Nigeria for medical treatment. Flew out on a Nigerian presidential jet.
  • As of April 2026: remains in Abuja. Trial has not formally proceeded. February 2025: Mansaray and Dumbuya travelled to Abuja to present Koroma with the new APC Digital ID — documenting continuing party stewardship from exile.

SLPP attack lines on Koroma

A presidential salary cannot buy a five-million-dollar house. The Commission found the money. The Court of Appeal found the men.

On 26 November 2023, his bodyguards walked into Wilberforce Barracks. Twenty Sierra Leoneans died. He has been in Abuja ever since.

He says he has retired. He still presents party ID cards to the Chairman from a hotel in Abuja.

↑ Top
PART III
Three Foundational Liabilities

Petroleum Directorate · New York Chancery · Aki-Sawyerr donor map

A · The Petroleum Directorate (COI Chapter 9)

It is the recommendation of this Commission that all monies and properties found to have been illegally acquired during the period under review be recovered.

— Justice Biobele Georgewill, Sole Commissioner of Inquiry

Figure · Chapter 9 Petroleum Directorate — recommended recoveries and related exposure.

  • Le 72.25 billion in local-currency recommended recovery (~USD 7.5M at 2018 rates).
  • USD 15.4 million in foreign-currency recommended recovery.
  • USD 14 million Rokel Commercial Bank loan — Court of Appeal upheld findings against Koroma and Raymond Kargbo; overturned against Kaifala Marah and Momodu Kargbo.
  • Raymond Kargbo — former Petroleum DG; Osho Coker rental scandal; findings upheld on appeal.
  • Mansaray (Chapter 8 Mines) — barred from public office for five years by White Paper recommendation.
  • Kaifala Marah — partially vindicated on appeal; remaining Chapter 9 exposures unresolved.

Mansaray ran our mines for ten years while billions disappeared. The Commission barred him for five years. He is now running for President.

The Commission found the money. The Court of Appeal found the men.

B · The New York Chancery Procurement Case

  • 2 November 2021: Six defendants indicted. USD 4.9 million total; USD 2.56 million attributed to Kamara.
  • February 2023: No-case ruling for two defendants. Remaining four proceed to full trial.
  • March 2023: Locus in quo (site visit) to New York — the court inspected the premises.
  • 12 July 2024: Closing arguments concluded. Judgement reserved.
  • October–November 2024: Kamara removed as National Leader under Section 47(a)(iv).
  • April 2026: Judgement remains reserved — a procedural anomaly for a case of this scale.
  • Defence counter-narrative: Ady Macauley's cross-examination of PW1 James Bockarie Noah elicited the Alie Kabba / ETIC SARL diversion testimony.

USD 4.9 million for an embassy. USD 2.56 million for one minister. The case is in court. The voters can wait for judgement; the party should not.

Risk warningIf the Chancery judgement is delivered as an acquittal before the February 2026 NDC, the entire SLPP attack on the Kamara faction collapses overnight. Prepare contingent responses for both outcomes.

C · The Aki-Sawyerr Donor and Partnership Map

Freetown is being run as a foreign-funded NGO with a flag.

— Internal SLPP framing draft, March 2026

  • Tier 1 — Sovereign donors: FCDO, World Bank (including official mayoral vehicle), Green Climate Fund (Freetown the Treetown).
  • Tier 2 — Foundation donors: Rockefeller Foundation, Bloomberg Philanthropies, Open Society Foundations.
  • Tier 3 — C40 and city networks: C40 Cities (Co-Chair since 2024), sister-city relationships with Charleston (USA) and Zurich (Switzerland).
  • Strongest pre-mayoral vulnerability: IDEA-UK / Cape Sierra Hotel / Hilton Worldwide concession (June 2010). Hotel demolished; project never completed. Requires documentary verification before deployment — current sourcing depends on APC-leaning Calabash Newspaper.
  • Live exposures: World Bank-funded official vehicle; 2022 obstruction-of-police charges (Sheku Turay arrest); February 2026 political boycott; March 2026 SLPP attempt to install Mayor Gbla as Acting Mayor.

When the Mayor of Freetown drives a car the World Bank bought her, she is not the Mayor of Freetown. She is the Mayor of Washington.

The Cape Sierra Hotel was demolished in 2010 for a Hilton that was never built. Where did our hotel go?

She is good at managing donors. She has not been good at managing Freetown.

↑ Top
PART IV
Tier 1 — Frontline Contenders

Jagaban · Aki-Sawyerr · Mansaray

TIER 1
Jagaban

Sheikh Alhaji Mohamed Omodu Kamara · Polling leader · Eastern crossover candidate · No governance record

His financial firepower is actively reshaping APC loyalties.

— Sierra Leone Telegraph, 'The APC Trap,' July 2025

Origin and bio

  • Born in Blama, Kenema District, Eastern Province — traditionally SLPP heartland. Father: Alhaji Amara Kamara, early APC pioneer in Kenema.
  • Education: Prince of Wales Secondary School (POW), Freetown; undergraduate and postgraduate in the UK and the USA.
  • Resides between Sierra Leone and the United States.

Career

  • Founder, Executive Director, Our Care Inc., Lanham, Maryland — nonprofit serving individuals with developmental and intellectual disabilities.
  • Sierra Leone business interests: Blama Pure Water Company; gas station chain; food processing factory along Waterloo–Mammah Highway.
  • Self-reported net worth: more than USD 50 million.
  • 2023: ran for Samura Kamara's running mate (VP candidate). Campaign site: mokjagaban.com.

Strengths

  • IGR December 2025 — only candidate across both parties above 50% endorsement.
  • Eastern Province heritage — unprecedented bridge for an APC candidate.
  • Cash-rich campaign infrastructure; no COI exposure; no governance record to defend.
  • Family APC pedigree in traditionally SLPP Kenema.

Vulnerabilities

  • No elected or appointed public office — ever. 'Can he run a country?' is structural.
  • Vote-buying framing — Sierra Leone Telegraph's 'APC Trap' describes him as buying loyalty with brown envelopes.
  • Self-reported USD 50M net worth; source-of-wealth scrutiny near-certain.
  • Significant US residency — 'has he lived the recent reality of Sierra Leone?'
  • Memphis allegation (March 2026) — Sahr Memphis accused Jagaban of plotting against him. No formal proceeding filed; unverified; do not deploy under SLPP attribution.

A man who has never held office is leading a poll because he has the most cash. That is not democracy — that is a transaction.

Where did the USD 50 million come from? Show us the accounts.

TIER 1
Yvonne Aki-Sawyerr

Mayor of Freetown · OBE · German Africa Award 2024 · C40 Co-Chair · See Part III C for full deep-report treatment

  • Mayor of Freetown since May 2018; re-elected June 2023.
  • OBE 2017 (Ebola response); TIME100 Climate; Forbes Sustainability Leaders; Schwab Outstanding Public Social Innovator.
  • Strongest pre-mayoral vulnerability: IDEA-UK / Cape Sierra Hotel / Hilton concession. Requires documentary verification before deployment.
  • Live exposures: World Bank-funded vehicle; 2022 obstruction-of-police charges; February 2026 political boycott; March 2026 Acting Mayor controversy.
  • Structural ceiling: Creole identity cuts against APC's Northern hegemony delegate base.

She is good at managing donors. She has not been good at managing Freetown.

TIER 1
Alhaji Minkailu Mansaray

National Chairman · NAC establishment vehicle · Ten years at Mines · Hajjgate acquittal

Mansaray, despite being Mines Minister for ten years and a senior APC figure, has never criticised Bio for all the problems his failed and barbaric SLPP regime had inflicted.

— APC Comrade Unisa Thorlu Conteh, public audio recording, 2025

  • Born Tonkolili District, Northern Province. Grew up in Freetown's East End. Temne. Muslim. Fourah Bay College graduate.
  • Sierra Leone National Insurance Company — worked alongside Ernest Bai Koroma. This is the foundational personal relationship structuring his entire political ascent.
  • MP 2002–2007 (Tonkolili). Minister of Labour 2007–2010. Minister of Mines and Mineral Resources 2010–2018 — ten unbroken years.
  • February 2023 APC Convention: elected National Chairman with 1,354 votes.
  • Hajjgate (July 2018): arrested with VP Foh over USD 80,000 missing from 2017 Hajj fund. 8 counts. Acquitted by Justice Reginald Sydney Fynn, October 2019.
  • COI Chapter 8: White Paper recommendation bars him from public office for five years.
  • 'Bio collaborator' allegation: APC Comrade Thorlu Conteh public audio — 'he has never criticised Bio.' Circulating inside the APC primary.

Ten years running our mines while billions disappeared. The Commission barred him for five years. He is now running for President.

Arrested with the Vice President for taking the pilgrims' money. The Court let him go. The voters can decide.

↑ Top
PART V
Tier 2 — Mid-Pack

Chericoco · Macauley · Konteh · Sam Sumana

Figure · APC 2023 convention result — the only available delegate-level data point.

TIER 2
Chericoco

Chernor Ramadan Maju Bah · Leader of the Opposition · Three-term MP · EBK custodian · Kenema wife

  • Born 29 April 1972. Lawyer — Solicitor and Barrister of Sierra Leone. Wife from Kenema District (Eastern Province) — a structural ethnic-coalition asset.
  • B&J Partners — his own law firm; Ady Macauley worked there before joining the ACC.
  • MP for Constituency 110/126 (Brookfields, Western Area) since 2007 — three consecutive terms. Re-elected 2012 with 68.45%.
  • Deputy Speaker of Parliament 2012–2017. 2018 APC Vice Presidential candidate (running mate to Samura Kamara). Leader of the Opposition since 2019.
  • Pioneered the Ernest Bai Koroma Trophy football tournament (2009) — credited with helping Koroma's 2012 re-election.
  • Section 47(a)(iv) issue: his term as Deputy National Leader reportedly expired alongside Kamara's.

He ran with Kamara in 2018. They lost. He ran with the same platform in 2023. They lost. Now he says he will be different.

He is Koroma 2.0. The COI record follows whoever the Koroma machine endorses.

TIER 2
Ady Macauley

Former ACC Commissioner · Defended Kamara in Chancery · Declared 18 December 2025

He touted convictions, yet critics pointed to gaps where APC big fish dodged scrutiny. He preferred minnows.

— Sierraeye Magazine, April 2025

  • Born 1 December 1978, Fourah Bay, Freetown. Creole. LLB Hons, University of London (2002). Called to the Bar of England and Wales.
  • B&J Partners (Chericoco's firm) before ACC. ACC Prosecutor from February 2011; rose to Chief of Prosecutions.
  • ACC Commissioner 2016–2018 — appointed by Koroma; replaced Joseph Fitzgerald Kamara (who became AG).
  • Lead defence counsel for Samura Kamara in the Chancery case — cross-examined PW1 to elicit the Alie Kabba / ETIC SARL diversion testimony.
  • 18 December 2025: launched 'Ady Macauley Movement' at Fourah Bay Field — attended by Sam Sumana, Jagaban, Timbo, Chericoco.

The Anti-Corruption Commissioner who let the big APC fish go free is now running to be their president.

TIER 2
Dr. Richard Konteh

Twice-cleared technocrat · Bombali · German PhD · 88 votes in 2023 · "D-UNIFIER"

Sierra Leone is not poor; it is poorly managed.

— Dr. Richard Konteh, AYV interview, 21 July 2025

  • Born 4 February 1963, Gbendembu Village, Bombali District. Christian (Wesleyan Church). German doctoral degree, University of Dortmund.
  • Catholic Relief Services, ECOWAS/UNDP Programme Manager, Njala University lecturer.
  • Deputy Minister of Finance 2007; Minister of Trade and Industry 2010; Chief of Staff to the President 2013.
  • June 2014: detained — CID evidence established it was Konteh's signature that was forged, not Konteh forging the President's. Charges withdrawn; nolle prosequi.
  • November 2022: ACC cleared him on the USD 11 million Japanese Non-Project Grant Aid (JNPGA).
  • February 2023 convention: 88 votes to Kamara's 1,382 — 5.8% of the delegate vote.
  • Currently declared 2028 aspirant. Campaign site: richardkonteh.com.

His own party gave him 88 votes out of 1,520. That is not a campaign — that is a hobby.

TIER 2
Sam Sumana

Alhaji Samuel Sidique Sam-Sumana · Former Vice President 2007–2015 · ECOWAS-vindicated · Kono base

Born APC, will die APC.

— Alhaji Samuel Sam-Sumana, Radisson Blu Freetown, 3 September 2025

  • Born 7 April 1962. Kono ethnicity, Eastern Province. Devout Muslim. Wife: Khadija Sam-Sumana. Previously resident in Minnesota, USA.
  • Pre-political: Managing Director, United Diamond Mining Company; Regional Manager, C-12 International (Texas).
  • VP 2007–2015. Reformed prisons into correctional centres; business registration reforms; mining-sector reform advocacy.
  • 6 March 2015: APC NAC expelled him. 17 March 2015: Koroma sacked him on TV. Replaced by Victor Bockarie Foh.
  • Sierra Leone Supreme Court (9 September 2015): five judges unanimously upheld the sacking.
  • ECOWAS Community Court (27 November 2017): ruled sacking illegal; violated human rights under African Charter. Lawyers: Femi Falana and Raymond Atuguba. Sierra Leone refused to participate and refused to recognise the judgement.
  • 2018: led C4C breakaway — won 8 parliamentary seats in Kono.
  • 3 September 2025: returned to APC. Readmitted (not reinstated) via Sec-Gen Dumbuya statement.
  • January 2026: attended APC leadership unity dialogue. Attended Macauley's December 2025 launch.

He left the APC, built his own party, lost with it, and now wants to lead the party he abandoned. That is not conviction — that is convenience.

The Sierra Leone Supreme Court was unanimous. Five judges. He had his hearing. He lost.

↑ Top
PART VI
Tier 3 — Wildcards

Blyden · Petito Koroma · Bangura · Timbo · Bayraytay

TIER 3
Dr. Sylvia Blyden

Medical doctor · Awareness Times founder · Former Social Welfare Minister · 22 days detained without warrant in 2020

  • Born 1 October 1971, Freetown. Creole. Christian. Great-great-granddaughter of Edward Wilmot Blyden — 'father of Pan-Africanism.'
  • MD in Medicine 1996, with proficiency. First woman elected Sierra Leone university students' leader (1994).
  • Founder, CEO, publisher of Awareness Times — only female-owned newspaper in Sierra Leone. 80,000+ Facebook followers.
  • Minister of Social Welfare, Gender and Children's Affairs under Koroma.
  • 1 May 2020: arrested by heavily armed police at home. No arrest warrant issued. Charged under Sections 27, 32, 33 of the 1965 Public Order Act — sedition, defamation, perversion of justice. Held at Freetown female correctional centre.
  • 28 May 2020: bail at Le 500 million bond + Le 1 billion sureties. 3 June 2020: re-arrested for allegedly violating bail. Total detention: ~22 days.
  • July 2020: High Court dismissed charges. July 2020: Parliament repealed Part V of the 1965 Public Order Act. November 2020: all magistrate charges dropped.
  • February 2021: Sierra Leone Police issued false INTERPOL notice claiming she had fled to Guinea. Police spokesperson confirmed document was authentic. No explanation given.
  • Thorlu Conteh 'Bio collaborator' allegation: same audio that named Mansaray named Blyden.
  • Best understood as a kingmaker through Awareness Times rather than a flagbearer.

The Bio government already showed its playbook against her: sedition charges, INTERPOL false notices, 22 days detained without warrant. Repeating it martyrs her further.

TIER 3
Alimamy Petito Koroma

Two-time failed flagbearer · Kambia District · Lomé Peace Accords mediator · SLPP-defection rumours

  • Kambia District. Born into a Muslim family; converted to Christianity. Wife: Rev. Linda Koroma (deceased).
  • Founder, Inter-Religious Council of Sierra Leone (1997). Key role in brokering Lomé Peace Accords (1999). Tanenbaum Center peacemaker recognition.
  • General Secretary, Council of Churches in Sierra Leone (CCSL).
  • MP Kambia 2002, 2007. Minister of Trade and Industry 2007 (managed 2007–08 food crisis via India rice imports). Minister of Works, Housing 2010. Sacked October 2013 alongside SLRA DG Munda Rogers — specific grounds not stated.
  • Ambassador to China March 2016. Resigned March 2017 for 'constitutional entanglements.'
  • Two failed flagbearer bids (2017–18 cycle; February 2023 convention). The Organiser (April 2025) included him in the 'fakers' list.
  • Persistent SLPP-defection rumours (Cocorioko, April 2023 — Temne Pentecostal Church Bio-praise event). Defection has not occurred.

His own party has rejected him twice. Once more and the hat-trick is complete.

TIER 3 → TIER 2 RECLASSIFICATION RECOMMENDED
Dr. Ibrahim Bangura — "DIB"

Oxford fellow · Two Routledge books · ECOWAS WPS Guidelines author · Konomanyi-endorsed March 2026

I believe in healing, uniting, and building. And I believe only a Sierra Leone that has confronted its own wounds can lead.

— Dr. Ibrahim Bangura, Paris, 22 April 2026

  • Born Kamakwie, Karene District (formerly Bombali), Northern Province. Raised in Freetown during the civil war.
  • PhD in Economics, HHL Leipzig Graduate School of Management, Germany.
  • Senior Lecturer, Department of Peace and Conflict Studies, Fourah Bay College, since 2008. Associate Professor 2022.
  • Africa Oxford Visiting Fellowship 2024/25 (with Dr. John Gledhill, Oxford International Development) — awarded to 'exceptional African scholars.'
  • Lectures at: KAIPTC (Ghana), Folke Bernadotte Academy (Sweden), Barcelona International Peace Centre.
  • 50+ peer-reviewed articles. Routledge books: 'Disarmament, Demobilisation and Reintegration of Ex-Combatants in Africa' (2023); 'Youth-Led Social Movements and Peacebuilding in Africa' (2022).
  • Authored ECOWAS Women, Peace and Security (WPS) Guidelines. Currently developing ECOWAS Gender Framework for Peace Support Operations.
  • First Sierra Leonean student to revive the APC student union at Fourah Bay College after the 1992 NPRC coup.
  • 23 March 2026: Diana Konomanyi publicly endorsed Bangura at Kailahun — hosted by APC Regional Chairman Stanley Moiwo Maya Kaikai. The most consequential factional endorsement of any non-Tier-1 candidate in the cycle.
  • No COI exposure. No prior public-office record to defend.
  • Vehicle: 'DIB Movement.' Mantra: 'Heal, Unite, Build.'
AssessmentBangura is the most underrated candidate in the field. His academic credentials are genuinely stronger than any recent Sierra Leonean presidential candidate from either party. The Konomanyi endorsement opens the East. The strongest SLPP attack is his foreign-network NGO career — but this also applies to Aki-Sawyerr. Monitor his convention progress closely; above 15% delegate share he becomes a real factor.
TIER 3
Hon. Osman Abdal Timbo

Sitting MP Constituency 130 · Helped draft 2022 APC Constitution · Son of former Chief Justice · Declared 1 May 2025

  • Father: late Alhaji Dr. Abdulai B. Timbo — former Chief Justice of Sierra Leone and Fullah Tribal Head for Freetown.
  • Lawyer. Devout Muslim. APC since 2013 — Western Area Regional Youth Leader circuit; National Executive Secretary, APC Youth League.
  • Elected MP for Constituency 130 (Western Area Urban) 2018. Parliamentary committees: Legislative (Deputy Chairman), PAC, Energy.
  • 2023: appointed Deputy National Secretary General. Helped draft the 2022 APC Constitution — including Section 47(a)(iv) itself.
  • April 2025: resigned Deputy SG role 'to ensure fairness within the party secretariat.' Pre-emptive conflict-of-interest avoidance.
  • 1 May 2025 (Labour Day): declared flagbearer at Brookfields New Hotel — IB Kargbo and Kasho-Holland Cole present.
  • Attended Macauley's December 2025 launch — inside the unity-dialogue track.
  • Most likely role: VP candidate or convention consensus figure rather than flagbearer front-runner.
  • Disambiguation: distinct from Alhaji Alpha Osman Timbo (SLPP, former Minister of Labour under Kabbah, 2011 SLPP flagbearer aspirant).
TIER 3
Abdulai Bayraytay

Former Presidential Spokesman under Koroma · Youth/civil-society appeal · Not yet formally declared

  • Former Presidential Spokesman under Ernest Bai Koroma.
  • Background: student union activism, civil society, journalism, public service.
  • Has not formally declared; described as 'consulting and quietly building support.'
  • Sierra Leone Telegraph: 'compelling oratory; connection with youth, the Fourth Estate, and grassroots movements; fresh yet formidable image.' 'Bayraytay's entrance could shake the foundation of the APC's political arithmetic.'
  • Best SLPP outcome: splits youth vote away from Jagaban or Macauley in the primary without winning himself.
↑ Top
PART VII
Tier 4 — Additional Aspirants

Joseph Fitzgerald Kamara · Konomanyi · Kaifala Marah · Palo Conteh

TIER 4
Joseph Fitzgerald Kamara — "JFK"

Former ACC Commissioner · Attorney-General 2016–2018 · Koroma's defence counsel in treason case

  • Born Makeni, Bombali District. LLB Hons, Fourah Bay College (1989). Masters in Law, Southern Methodist University, Texas (2000).
  • Deputy Prosecutor, Special Court for Sierra Leone — prosecuted Charles Taylor; pioneer in recognising forced marriage as a crime under international law.
  • ACC Commissioner 2010–2015: lifted Sierra Leone's TI CPI ranking from 134th to 119th; convicted three ministers, Mayor of Freetown, Head of NRA, Head of National Maritime Agency.
  • World Bank Integrity Award 2010. Joint winner, International Association of Prosecutors Award.
  • January 2016: appointed Attorney-General by Koroma — became the first AG to publicly hand in his asset declaration form to the ACC.
  • 2018 flagbearer aspirant: NAC disqualified him on grounds that his APC membership had been backdated. Koroma anointed Kamara.
  • Current role: Koroma's lead defence counsel in the treason case. His statement that 'Ernest Koroma was forced to leave Sierra Leone; he didn't leave by choice' is the cornerstone of the EBK exile framing.

He built his anti-corruption reputation on catching small fish. Then he became the lawyer defending the man charged with the biggest corruption of them all.

TIER 4
Diana Konomanyi

"Iron Lady of Kono" · APC Eastern Province Chairperson 2005–2022 · COI conviction overturned 2021 · Endorsed Bangura March 2026

  • Born and raised Koidu Town, Kono District. Father: Fatoma Decius Konomanyi — leading APC figure and Mayor of Koidu New Sembehun City Council.
  • APC since 1996. Businesses: hotelier/restaurateur; contract services; diamond/gold mining.
  • Chairperson, Kono District Council 2008–2012 — first female District Council Chairperson in Kono.
  • Minister of Local Government and Rural Development 2012–2015 (first woman in this post). Minister of Lands, Country Planning and the Environment 2015–2018.
  • APC Eastern Province Chairperson 2005–2022 — nearly two decades.
  • COI findings: conviction and premises-confiscation recommended. Court of Appeal (Justice Fatmata Bintu Alhadi, June 2021): overturned; premises recommendation set aside. ACC directed to prosecute under Section 119 of the ACC Act (asset declaration) — status unconfirmed.
  • 2023 elections: ostracised by APC hierarchy under Kamara; house in Koidu reportedly targeted (arson attempt). Her political withdrawal immediately weakened APC's Eastern performance.
  • 23 March 2026: publicly endorsed Dr. Ibrahim Bangura at Kailahun — the first serious Eastern factional break. SLPP must track whether this widens.
TIER 4
Dr. Kaifala Marah

Former Finance Minister 2013–2016 · Bank Governor 2016–2017 · Kuranko · Partially vindicated by Court of Appeal

  • Born Koidu Town, Kono District. Family from Koinadugu District. Kuranko ethnicity. Muslim.
  • BA International Relations, Fourah Bay College. MA Public Policy, SUNY. Doctorate (political economy), University of Hull.
  • Chief of Staff and Senior Economic Advisor to Koroma 2010–2012. Minister of Finance 2012–2016. Governor, Bank of Sierra Leone 2016–2017.
  • COI: named in Chapter 9 Petroleum Directorate findings. Court of Appeal overturned findings against him on the Rokel loan — Ministry of Finance found to have operated within statutory mandates. Remaining exposures unresolved.
  • 7 March 2017: resigned as Bank Governor to seek the 2018 APC presidential nomination — bid unsuccessful.
  • Campaign site: kaifalamarah.com. Listed as 'Minister of Foreign Affairs' on campaign site and LinkedIn — not found in official records; verify before citing.
  • Aligned with the Kamara-loyalist faction.

He served as Finance Minister, then Bank Governor, then resigned to run for president. The Commission found the petroleum money went missing on his watch. The Court let him go on a technicality.

TIER 4
Rtd. Major Alfred Paolo Conteh — "Palo Conteh"

Former Defence and Internal Affairs Minister · National Ebola Response Coordinator · Acquitted of treason July 2020

  • Retired Major, Republic of Sierra Leone Armed Forces. Qualified lawyer.
  • Served as Defence Minister and Internal Affairs Minister under Koroma. National Ebola Response Coordinator 2014–2016.
  • 19 March 2020: entered State House with licensed personal pistol to attend a COVID-19 consultation. Security detected the gun after he bypassed the scanner. Bio government charged him with 16 counts including treason.
  • 1 July 2020: Jury (12 members) unanimously acquitted on all treason charges. Convicted on two lesser counts of unlawful gun possession — sentenced to two years.
  • Lead defence: Dr. Abdulai O. Conteh (former Chief Justice of Belize); support: Ady Macauley, Joseph Fitzgerald Kamara.
  • The jury acquittal is a landmark — widely cited as evidence the judiciary stood firm against executive pressure.
  • SLPP guidance: attacking the treason case amplifies his martyrdom narrative. Focus on his Koroma-era defence portfolio rather than the 2020 case.
↑ Top
PART VIII
Cross-Cutting Strategic Issues

Six dynamics that cut across all candidate profiles

1 · Section 47(a)(iv) — the 2022 APC Constitution

Section 47(a)(iv) of the 2022 APC Constitution requires a flagbearer who loses a general election to vacate the National Leader position within six months of that loss. Samura Kamara was removed in October–November 2024. Chericoco's Deputy National Leader role reportedly expired on the same basis. Whoever wins the February 2026 convention automatically becomes National Leader — but a 2032 loss fires the same provision. This creates a recurring structural trap and makes the convention prize both larger and more conditional than it appears. Timbo, as one of the Constitution's drafters, uniquely understands this architecture.

2 · Readmission vs reinstatement (Sumana case)

Sec-Gen Dumbuya's 5 September 2025 statement framed Sam Sumana's return as readmission via due process — implicitly preserving the legal effect of his 2015 NAC expulsion and the 2015 Supreme Court sacking. If the establishment NAC enforces a 'readmitted member ineligible for senior office' reading, Sumana is blocked from the flagbearer race without a single vote being cast.

3 · The 2023 election dispute

The APC's single strongest argument is the 2023 credibility deficit. Carter Center: not confident the results accurately reflected the will of the people. EU EOM: statistical inconsistencies. NEW's PRVT: Bio did not clear the 55% threshold. US, UK, Ireland, Germany, France: joint statement of concern. The SLPP must either acknowledge and distinguish these findings or risk its credibility with international and educated domestic audiences. The strategic recommendation: separate the question of 2023 legitimacy from the question of APC capacity to govern. Lead with the COI record; do not relitigate 2023.

4 · The Hawa Dumbuya death

On 25 June 2023, security forces fired tear gas and reportedly live ammunition into the APC headquarters during a post-election press conference. Hawa Dumbuya, a nurse and APC volunteer, was severely wounded and died from her injuries. The Hawa Dumbuya death is a live grievance with real electoral weight in APC base communities that no SLPP communications strategy can ignore.

5 · The ITGC challenge

The Interim Transition and Governance Committee, led by Alfred Peter Conteh, continues to challenge the legitimacy of the current APC executive, citing a ruling by Justice Hannah Bonnie. As of April 2026 the challenge is unresolved. If a court ruling invalidates the current executive, the entire flagbearer process becomes contested. Low-probability, high-impact tail risk.

6 · The Koroma–Kamara alliance fracture

Reporting from the Sierra Leone Telegraph, Cocorioko, AYV News, and Sierraloaded indicates the structural alliance between Koroma and Kamara has fractured. Koroma is reportedly withholding the backing that produced Kamara's 1,382-vote convention victory in 2023 and is open to other contenders. The question of which Tier 1 candidate receives Koroma's active or passive blessing is the single most consequential variable between now and the convention.

↑ Top
PART IX
Vulnerability Matrix and Attack Inventory

12 candidates × 7 attack vectors — the complete SLPP attack reference

Figure · Vulnerability matrix — 12 APC aspirants × 7 attack vectors. Calibrated from open-source research.

  • Severe (dark red): Directly deployable. Documented, sourced, non-speculative.
  • High (red): Deployable with care — substantive but requires sourcing discipline.
  • Moderate (pink): Real vulnerability but needs additional documentary verification or framing.
  • Low (light): Present as a line of inquiry but not reliably deployable without additional research.
  • — (grey): No credible attack line in this vector based on available open-source research.

Lines that work across the entire APC

Under the APC, our mines, our petroleum, our rutile — they were all looted. The Commission proved it. The Court of Appeal confirmed the key findings.

They lost 2018. They lost 2023. They are now fighting over who gets to lose 2028 on behalf of the same network that ran the country into the ground.

The man they chose in 2023 is under a USD 4.9 million indictment. The judgement has been reserved for nearly two years. They cannot even explain that.

Lines targeting specific candidates

  • Jagaban: A man who has never held office is leading a poll because he has the most cash. That is not democracy — that is a transaction.
  • Mansaray: Ten years running our mines while billions disappeared. The Commission barred him for five years. He is now running for President.
  • Chericoco: He ran with Kamara in 2018. They lost. He ran with the same platform in 2023. They lost. Now he says he will be different.
  • Macauley: The Anti-Corruption Commissioner who let the big APC fish go free is now running to be their president.
  • Konteh: His own party gave him 88 votes out of 1,520. That is not a campaign — that is a hobby.
  • Sam Sumana: He left the APC, built his own party, lost with it, and now wants to lead the party he abandoned. That is not conviction — that is convenience.
  • Aki-Sawyerr: She is good at managing donors. She has not been good at managing Freetown.
  • JFK: He built his anti-corruption reputation on catching small fish. Then he became the lawyer defending the man charged with the biggest corruption of them all.
  • Koroma (backdrop): On 26 November 2023, his bodyguards walked into Wilberforce Barracks. Twenty Sierra Leoneans died. He has been in Abuja ever since.

Lines to avoid

  • Attacking the 2023 election result directly. The APC's strongest argument is the Carter Center / EU EOM documentation. Every SLPP attack on the 2023 result amplifies the APC's best defence.
  • The Memphis allegation against Jagaban. Unverified; no proceeding filed; no public response. High-risk of backfiring.
  • Koroma-era attacks that ignore the vindication record. Multiple COI findings overturned on appeal. Any attack that treats all findings as final facts will be rebutted with specific acquittal dates and names.
  • Heavy-handed attacks on Blyden or Palo Conteh. Both are APC martyrdom figures; attacking them amplifies their victim narratives.
↑ Top

Sierra Leone Supreme Court — Sam Sumana v Government of Sierra Leone (9 September 2015). Five-judge unanimous ruling. Justice Vivian Solomon lead opinion. Physical record: Judiciary, Freetown.

High Court — State v. Dr. Samura Kamara and five others (Chancery). Indicted 2 November 2021. Closing arguments 12 July 2024. Judgement reserved. High Court, Freetown.

High Court — State v. Mansaray and others (Hajjgate). Acquittal by Justice Fynn, October 2019. High Court, Freetown.

High Court — State v. Rtd. Major Palo Conteh (treason). Jury acquittal 1 July 2020. High Court, Freetown.

High Court — State v. Dr. Sylvia Blyden (sedition). Charges dismissed July 2020. High Court, Freetown.

Koroma treason case (4 charges). Charged 3 January 2024. Travel to Nigeria permitted late January 2024. Trial not formally proceeded as of April 2026. High Court, Freetown.

11Master Legal Caveats
Read before any deploymentAll COI findings are administrative recommendations, not adjudicated convictions. The Court of Appeal has overturned a meaningful subset. Koroma remains presumed innocent. The Chancery judgement is still reserved. Sam Sumana's ECOWAS ruling is genuine but refused by Sierra Leone since 2017. Palo Conteh was acquitted on all treason charges. Konomanyi's COI conviction was overturned. Mansaray was acquitted in Hajjgate. Konteh was twice cleared.
12Recommended Supplementary Research
Full text of the Konomanyi Kailahun endorsement (March 2026) — verify specific language and attendees.
IDEA-UK / Cape Sierra Hotel / Hilton concession documents — required before the Cape Sierra line can be deployed.
October 2013 Petito Koroma / Munda Rogers simultaneous sacking — the SLRA procurement angle is under-researched.
Full Constitution-drafting committee membership of the 2022 APC document.
Dr. Bangura's ethnic background (Limba/Temne/other) — not confirmed in available reporting.
Petito Koroma's specific 2018 and 2023 flagbearer vote totals.
Dr. Kaifala Marah's "Minister of Foreign Affairs" claim on his campaign site — not found in official records; verify before citing.
